subject: Science
chapter: The Amazing World of Solute, Solvent, and Solutions

key_points: A solution is a homogeneous mixture of two or more substances
The substance that dissolves is called the solute
The substance that dissolves the solute is called the solvent
Water is commonly called the universal solvent
Solutions can exist in solid, liquid, or gaseous states
examples: Salt dissolved in water forms a salt solution
Sugar dissolved in water forms a sugar solution
Air is a solution of gases
Carbon dioxide dissolved in water forms soda water
Brass is a solid solution of copper and zinc
formulas_or_processes: Solute: Substance that dissolves in a solvent
Solvent: Substance that dissolves the solute
Solution: Homogeneous mixture of solute and solvent
Dissolving: Process in which solute particles mix uniformly in solvent
exam_tips: Remember the difference between solute, solvent, and solution
Learn common examples of solutions in daily life
Understand that solutions are homogeneous mixtures
Identify the solvent as the component present in larger amount
